extraction.one_line_summary: VAN BAARLE attacked from breakaway on final climb, held off HAIG in two-up sprint after ALAPHILIPPE bridged but couldn't contest finale.
tactical_patterns: HAIG's team role shifted from passive to active once YATES abandoned; opportunistic breakaway tactics depend on GC team status.
ALAPHILIPPE bridged aggressively on penultimate climb but sat up 41s back on final ascent, prioritizing KOM jersey over stage win.
ASTANA paced entire climb at 'prolific tempo' to nullify attacks, defensive domestique work more effective than expected GC aggression.
VAN BAARLE sprinted very early in finale with 300m remaining, long sprint successfully held off stronger finisher HAIG.

rider_signals.YATES Adam: negative: abandoned while 2nd overall due to sickness/fever, illustrating vulnerability after cold/wet stage exposure day prior.
rider_signals.KRUIJSWIJK Steven: negative: abandoned mid-stage, 9th overall, also appearing to suffer illness effects from previous day's conditions.
rider_signals.HAIG Jack: positive: immediately activated and contributed to breakaway after YATES abandoned, finishing 2nd shows tactical flexibility and strong form.
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: neutral: bridged to leaders aggressively but abandoned stage ambitions 1km out, calculated KOM jersey prioritization over stage hunting.
rider_signals.VAN BAARLE Dylan: positive: won from breakaway with very early sprint timing, stronger than expected against specialist finishers in uphill finale.
rider_signals.HAGEN Carl Fredrik: positive: 3rd place in first World Tour season from breakaway, unexpected strength in hilly stage finale for young rider.
rider_signals.BARGUIL Warren: positive: 4th place marked improvement, showing better form than earlier Dauphiné performances in selective finale.
rider_signals.PINOT Thibaut: neutral: attacked multiple times on final climb but Astana's pace nullified moves, testing form but unable to distance FUGLSANG.
rider_signals.FUGLSANG Jakob: positive: defended yellow with ease despite PINOT attacks, Astana's collective pacing strategy eliminated all GC threats without personal effort.
